libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Write the generated test as Scala 3 source, not Java-only code. Import `io.circe.literal._` and exercise the `json"..."` interpolator with literals and interpolated values so the compile-time macro expands into runtime `io.circe.Json` values. Avoid adding parser/test-framework dependencies just to compare results; assertions can use `Json` APIs such as `hcursor`, `noSpaces`, `Json.fromInt`, `Json.obj`, and related circe-core methods already available transitively.
risks: A Java-only scaffold will not reach the main public API because `circe-literal_3` exposes a Scala 3 string-interpolator extension backed by macros.
Most library-specific behavior happens at Scala compile time, so native-image runtime coverage may be small even with correct tests; local Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency, Docker image, or environment setting is needed; the artifact brings its Circe/Jawn dependencies transitively, but meaningful coverage requires Scala 3 test code that invokes the literal macro.
